summary: Verona is where my mother, Deborah, was born in 1969 and where she returned to live with my grandparents after college. It was the first place my parents lived together as a couple, although it was challenging because my dad struggled to find work and faced visa issues. Living in Verona with my mom’s parents was a significant part of our family’s early story before we moved to the United States.

Italy is my mother’s homeland where she grew up in Vicenza and attended the University of Verona. It’s the country where my parents first met during their exchange programs and where they initially tried to settle down. Although we moved away, Italy remains an important part of our heritage, and we used to visit every year to celebrate Italian holidays and stay connected with family.

Vicenza is the small town near Venice where my mother was raised along with her two brothers. It was a comfortable upbringing supported by my grandfather’s model train business. Growing up in Vicenza shaped much of my mother’s early life before she went to college in Verona.

Venice is the famous city near where my mother grew up in Vicenza. Although we didn’t live in Venice itself, it was close enough to be part of the regional culture and geography that influenced my mother’s early years. It’s a landmark reference point in her story.

My mother attended the University of Verona, where she started her higher education journey. It was during her time there that she decided to participate in an exchange program to Lyon, France, which led to meeting my father. The University of Verona was the starting point of her international experience and eventual immigration story.

Lyon, in France, is where my mother and father met during their exchange programs in college. My mother was studying there from the University of Verona, and my father came from the University of Georgia. Their relationship began in Lyon and continued through letters and phone calls after they left.
cues: University of Verona and University of Georgia

summary: France, specifically Lyon, played a pivotal role in my parents’ story as the place where they met while on exchange programs. It was a cultural crossroads for them, connecting my mother from Italy and my father from the United States. Their time in France marked the beginning of their long-distance relationship.

My father attended the University of Georgia before going on an exchange program to Lyon, France, where he met my mother. He was born in Denver, Colorado, and later worked for the Peace Corps in Bulgaria. The University of Georgia was an important part of his early academic and international experience.

Denver, Colorado, is where my father was born to Michael and Sandy Allen. It’s part of his American roots before he embarked on his international experiences and eventually moved to Alaska and Oregon. Denver represents the beginning of his family’s story in the United States.

Colorado is the state where my father was born, specifically in Denver. It’s part of his American background before he moved around for work and family reasons. Colorado is where his parents lived before the family eventually relocated to Alaska and Oregon.

My father worked for the Peace Corps in Bulgaria, which was a significant chapter in his life. It was during this time that he even hitchhiked to Italy to visit my mother, showing how committed he was to their relationship. The Peace Corps experience reflects his adventurous and service-oriented spirit.

Bulgaria is where my father was stationed while working for the Peace Corps. It was from there that he made the memorable journey hitchhiking to Italy to see my mother. Bulgaria represents an important part of his international service and the lengths he went to for family.

Anchorage, Alaska, is where my parents moved after struggling to live in Italy. It was a big change for my mother, coming from Italy to the cold and different culture of Alaska. My mother got a job at Nordstrom’s there, but eventually, they decided it was too cold and moved to Portland, Oregon.

Alaska was the first place my parents settled in the United States, specifically Anchorage, where my father’s parents lived. It was a stark contrast to Italy in terms of weather and culture. Although my mother found work there, the cold climate eventually prompted us to move to Portland, Oregon.

My mother found work at Nordstrom’s in Anchorage, Alaska, shortly after getting her green card through marriage. This job was important for her as she adjusted to life in the United States. However, despite the job, the cold climate made them decide to relocate to Portland, Oregon.

Portland, Oregon, is where my family settled after leaving Alaska because the cold was too much for us. My mother eventually gained full U.S. citizenship there, and it’s where I was born in 2000. Portland became our home and the place where we started to adapt more fully to American life.

The United States is where my father was born and where my family eventually settled after moving from Italy and Alaska. It’s where I and my sister were born and raised, and where we gradually lost some of our Italian language and traditions. Despite this, we still maintain ties to our heritage and hope to reconnect through language and study abroad.
